steps.title: Set up constraints and minimize x to maximize y
steps.content:
Let $x$ = number of 80-pound packages and $y$ = number of 95-pound packages.

Two constraints apply:
  • At least 12 packages: $x + y \geq 12$
  • Weight limit: $80x + 95y \leq 1050$
To maximize $y$, we minimize $x$. From the first constraint, the minimum $x$ for a given $y$ is $x = 12 - y$.

steps.title: Solve for the maximum value of y
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 12 - y$ into the weight constraint:
$$80(12 - y) + 95y \leq 1050$$
$$960 - 80y + 95y \leq 1050$$
$$960 + 15y \leq 1050$$
$$15y \leq 90$$
$$y \leq 6$$
The maximum is $y = 6$. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
